perturbation.original_text: The PARTIES hereby agree that the DISCLOSING PARTY shall retain ownership and control over any CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION disclosed pursuant to this AGREEMENT. The RECEIVING PARTIES shall not by reason of any disclosure made pursuant to this AGREEMENT thereby obtain any rights with respect to such CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION other than the right of the PARTY, or an ENTITY that the PARTY assigns its rights to under this Agreement in compliance with Section 5 herein, to use the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION for the purposes of NRSC analysis only, and agree that they will not: (i) use such CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION for any 2 reason other than NRSC analysis only as expressly authorized by this Agreement; or (ii) sell, lend, lease, distribute, license, give, transfer, assign, show, disclose, disseminate, appropriate, or otherwise communicate any such CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION to any person or ENTITY outside the RECEIVING PARTY or its assignee under this Agreement, except as expressly authorized under this AGREEMENT.
perturbation.changed_text: The PARTIES hereby agree that the DISCLOSING PARTY shall retain ownership and control over any CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION disclosed pursuant to this AGREEMENT. The RECEIVING PARTIES shall not by reason of any disclosure made pursuant to this AGREEMENT thereby obtain any rights with respect to such CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION other than the right of the PARTY to use the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION for the purposes of NRSC analysis only, and agree that they will not: (i) use such CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION for any 2 reason other than NRSC analysis only as expressly authorized by this Agreement; or (ii) sell, lend, lease, distribute, license, give, transfer, assign, show, disclose, disseminate, appropriate, or otherwise communicate any such CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION to any person or ENTITY outside the RECEIVING PARTY or its assignee under this Agreement, except as expressly authorized under this AGREEMENT.
perturbation.explanation: Removing 'or an ENTITY that the PARTY assigns its rights to under this Agreement in compliance with Section 5 herein' contradicts Section 5, which explicitly allows for assignment of rights under the agreement. Now, the text implies the receiving party cannot assign the rights, contradicting what Section 5 states.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Binding Effect and Assignability . The rights and obligations of each PARTY under this AGREEMENT shall inure to the benefit of and shall be binding upon any subsidiary, affiliate, successor or permitted assign of or to the business of such PARTY, to the extent provided below. Neither this AGREEMENT nor any rights or obligations of a PARTY under this AGREEMENT shall be transferable or assignable by that PARTY without the prior written consent of all other PARTIES, and any attempted transfer or assignment of this 3 AGREEMENT by a PARTY not in accordance herewith shall be null and void. Notwith standing the foregoing provisions of this Section, a PARTY may assign this AGREEMENT immediately, without the prior written consent of all other PARTIES, (a) to any ENTITY that c ontrols, is controlled by, or is in common control with the assigning PARTY, ( b) to any successor in interest to the assigning PARTY, or (c) if necessary to satisfy the rules, regulations and/or orders of any federal, state or local governmental agency or body. The co nfidentiality obligations and remedies set forth in this AGREEMEN T, including without limitation, those set for in Sections 3, 4, 5, 6 and 7 shall bind any successor to any PARTY. Notwithstanding the foregoing, each PARTY that receives or obtains CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION pursuant to the terms of this AGREEMENT shall re main bound by all confidentiality obligations, and subject to all remedies, under this AGREEMENT after making an assignment or transfer, including, without limitation, the obligations set forth in Sections 3, 6, 7 and 8 and the remedies specified in Sectio n 4.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the phrase 'or an ENTITY that the PARTY assigns its rights to under this Agreement in compliance with Section 5 herein' from the RECEIVING PARTIES rights. This directly contradicts the 'Binding Effect and Assignability' section which explicitly allows assignment of rights under certain conditions, creating an in-text contradiction as defined by the Omissions - In Text Contradiction perturbation type.

perturbation.original_text: The PARTIES further agree that, except as expressly authorized herein, no CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION will be disclosed to any governmental ENTITY, officer or employee without first obtaining the express written consent of the DISCLOSING PARTY. 7. Termination.
perturbation.changed_text: The PARTIES further agree that, except as expressly authorized herein, no CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION will be disclosed to any governmental ENTITY, officer or employee . 7. Termination.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'without first obtaining the express written consent of the DISCLOSING PARTY', the contract now implies that no CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION can EVER be disclosed to a governmental entity. This contradicts Section 3(f), which outlines a process for disclosing confidential information under court order, potentially involving governmental entities.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (f) Except as specified in this Subsection, nothing herein shall preclude a PARTY who b ecomes legally obligated by any governmental ENTITY with jurisdiction over it from complying with a court order or other valid legal process compelling disclosure of CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION. If a PARTY receives a court order, subpoena, document demand, o r other order or process compelling or seeking disclosure of CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION, such PARTY shall provide the DISCLOSING PARTY with prompt written notice of such demand or request, and, in any event, before any CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION is disclosed. The PARTY served with such demand, order or process shall resist disclosure on grounds of this AGREEMENT, and shall take all reasonable steps to provide the DISCLOSING PARTY a full and fair opportunity, prior to disclosure, to resist, oppose or otherwise limit any disclosure of such CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION. The PARTY will disclose only such information as is legally required and will use commercially reasonable efforts to obtain confidential treatment for any CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION that is s o disclosed.
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed disclosure of confidential information to governmental entities with the DISCLOSING PARTY's express written consent. The changed text removes this condition, implying that no CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION can EVER be disclosed to a governmental entity. This directly contradicts Section 3(f), which outlines a process for disclosing confidential information under court order, potentially involving governmental entities, thereby creating a strong in-text contradiction because it breaks compatibility between the clauses.

perturbation.original_text: This AGREEMENT shall expire on Dece mber 31, 2017 . Notwithstanding the foregoing, each PARTY that receives or obtains CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION pursuant to the terms of this AGREEMENT shall remain bound for five (5) years from the date of disclosure by all confidentiality obligations, and su bject to all remedies, under this AGREEMENT after termination, including, without limitation, the obligations set forth in Sections 3 , 6 and 7 and the remedies specified in Section 4. Upon written request, all CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION shall be returned to the DISCLOSING PARTY or destroyed at the request of the DISCLOSING PARTY. Similarly, if at any time prior to December 31, 2017 , a PARTY withdraws from the NRSC , any DISCLOSING PARTY may request that the withdrawing PARTY, and the withdrawing PARTY may re quest that the RECEIVING PARTY either return all CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION to the DISCLOSING PARTY or destroy such information. Within seven (7) days of receipt of a written notice requesting return or destruction of any CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION, the RECEI VING PARTY shall, as requested, either return or destroy all designated CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION and all copies of such CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION, whether in written or electronic form.
perturbation.changed_text: This AGREEMENT shall expire on Dece mber 31, 2017 . Notwithstanding the foregoing, each PARTY that receives or obtains CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION pursuant to the terms of this AGREEMENT shall remain bound for five (5) years from the date of disclosure by all confidentiality obligations, and su bject to all remedies, under this AGREEMENT after termination, including, without limitation, the obligations set forth in Sections 3 and 6 and the remedies specified in Section 4. Upon written request, all CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION shall be returned to the DISCLOSING PARTY or destroyed at the request of the DISCLOSING PARTY. Similarly, if at any time prior to December 31, 2017 , a PARTY withdraws from the NRSC , any DISCLOSING PARTY may request that the withdrawing PARTY, and the withdrawing PARTY may re quest that the RECEIVING PARTY either return all CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION to the DISCLOSING PARTY or destroy such information. Within seven (7) days of receipt of a written notice requesting return or destruction of any CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION, the RECEI VING PARTY shall, as requested, either return or destroy all designated CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION and all copies of such CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION, whether in written or electronic form.
perturbation.explanation: By removing Section 7 from the sections that remain in effect after termination, it contradicts the second sentence of the same section. That second sentence stating that obligations in 'Section 7' survive after the expiration of the agreement. Because we deleted it on the first sentence, it creates and in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This AGREEMENT shall expire on Dece mber 31, 2017 . Notwithstanding the foregoing, each PARTY that receives or obtains CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION pursuant to the terms of this AGREEMENT shall remain bound for five (5) years from the date of disclosure by all confidentiality obligations, and su bject to all remedies, under this AGREEMENT after termination, including, without limitation, the obligations set forth in Sections 3 and 6 and the remedies specified in Section 4. Upon written request, all CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION shall be returned to the DISCLOSING PARTY or destroyed at the request of the DISCLOSING PARTY. Similarly, if at any time prior to December 31, 2017 , a PARTY withdraws from the NRSC , any DISCLOSING PARTY may request that the withdrawing PARTY, and the withdrawing PARTY may re quest that the RECEIVING PARTY either return all CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION to the DISCLOSING PARTY or destroy such information. Within seven (7) days of receipt of a written notice requesting return or destruction of any CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION, the RECEI VING PARTY shall, as requested, either return or destroy all designated CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION and all copies of such CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION, whether in written or electronic form.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that Sections 3, 6, and 7 remain in effect after termination. The changed text omits Section 7 from this list, meaning obligations under Section 7 no longer survive termination according to the first sentence. However, the same section (which is also the contradicted text) contains a second sentence that implies obligations in Section 7 should survive after the expiration of the agreement. This creates a direct in-text contradiction because the section references 'Section 7' even though, according to the modified first sentence, it should not be considered when the agreement expires. This is an omission that leads to an in-text contradiction based on the definition provided.

perturbation.original_text: The rights and obligations of each PARTY under this AGREEMENT shall inure to the benefit of and shall be binding upon any subsidiary, affiliate, successor or permitted assign of or to the business of such PARTY, to the extent provided below. Neither this AGREEMENT nor any rights or obligations of a PARTY under this AGREEMENT shall be transferable or assignable by that PARTY without the prior written consent of all other PARTIES, and any attempted transfer or assignment of this 3 AGREEMENT by a PARTY not in accordance herewith shall be null and void.
perturbation.changed_text: The rights and obligations of each PARTY under this AGREEMENT shall inure to the benefit of and shall be binding upon any subsidiary, affiliate, successor or permitted assign of or to the business of such PARTY. Neither this AGREEMENT nor any rights or obligations of a PARTY under this AGREEMENT shall be transferable or assignable by that PARTY without the prior written consent of all other PARTIES, and any attempted transfer or assignment of this 3 AGREEMENT by a PARTY not in accordance herewith shall be null and void.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'to the extent provided below', it suggests that the rights and obligations are *always* binding upon subsidiaries, affiliates, successors, etc. This contradicts the following sentences in that paragraph that specify the conditions and limitations on assignment, specifically how it can only happen with prior consent.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Binding Effect and Assignability . The rights and obligations of each PARTY under this AGREEMENT shall inure to the benefit of and shall be binding upon any subsidiary, affiliate, successor or permitted assign of or to the business of such PARTY. Neither this AGREEMENT nor any rights or obligations of a PARTY under this AGREEMENT shall be transferable or assignable by that PARTY without the prior written consent of all other PARTIES, and any attempted transfer or assignment of this 3 AGREEMENT by a PARTY not in accordance herewith shall be null and void. Notwith standing the foregoing provisions of this Section, a PARTY may assign this AGREEMENT immediately, without the prior written consent of all other PARTIES, (a) to any ENTITY that c ontrols, is controlled by, or is in common control with the assigning PARTY, ( b) to any successor in interest to the assigning PARTY, or (c) if necessary to satisfy the rules, regulations and/or orders of any federal, state or local governmental agency or body. The co nfidentiality obligations and remedies set forth in this AGREEMEN T, including without limitation, those set for in Sections 3, 4, 5, 6 and 7 shall bind any successor to any PARTY. Notwithstanding the foregoing, each PARTY that receives or obtains CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION pursuant to the terms of this AGREEMENT shall re main bound by all confidentiality obligations, and subject to all remedies, under this AGREEMENT after making an assignment or transfer, including, without limitation, the obligations set forth in Sections 3, 6, 7 and 8 and the remedies specified in Sectio n 4.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that rights and obligations are binding upon subsidiaries, affiliates, successors, etc., 'to the extent provided below'. This implies limitations on the binding effect. The changed text removes 'to the extent provided below,' suggesting that the rights and obligations are *always* binding. This creates a direct contradiction because the contradicted text provides specific conditions and limitations on assignment, including the requirement of prior written consent and exceptions where assignment can happen without consent. The removal makes the first sentence an absolute statement, while the subsequent sentences provide exceptions, creating an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: If a PARTY receives a court order, subpoena, document demand, o r other order or process compelling or seeking disclosure of CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION, such PARTY shall provide the DISCLOSING PARTY with prompt written notice of such demand or request, and, in any event, before any CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION is disclosed. The PARTY served with such demand, order or process shall resist disclosure on grounds of this AGREEMENT, and shall take all reasonable steps to provide the DISCLOSING PARTY a full and fair opportunity, prior to disclosure, to resist, oppose or otherwise limit any disclosure of such CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION.
perturbation.changed_text: If a PARTY receives a court order, subpoena, document demand, o r other order or process compelling or seeking disclosure of CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION, such PARTY shall provide the DISCLOSING PARTY with prompt written notice of such demand or request. The PARTY served with such demand, order or process shall resist disclosure on grounds of this AGREEMENT, and shall take all reasonable steps to provide the DISCLOSING PARTY a full and fair opportunity, prior to disclosure, to resist, oppose or otherwise limit any disclosure of such CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'and, in any event, before any CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION is disclosed', the agreement no longer explicitly requires the receiving party to notify the disclosing party before disclosing confidential information under a court order, which leads to an in-text contradiction. While it says to provide prompt written notice, it does not enforce it that the party can not disclose the confidential information. This contradicts the intent of protecting confidential information and the emphasis on providing the disclosing party an opportunity to resist disclosure
perturbation.contradicted_text: (f) Except as specified in this Subsection, nothing herein shall preclude a PARTY who b ecomes legally obligated by any governmental ENTITY with jurisdiction over it from complying with a court order or other valid legal process compelling disclosure of CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION. If a PARTY receives a court order, subpoena, document demand, o r other order or process compelling or seeking disclosure of CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION, such PARTY shall provide the DISCLOSING PARTY with prompt written notice of such demand or request. The PARTY served with such demand, order or process shall resist disclosure on grounds of this AGREEMENT, and shall take all reasonable steps to provide the DISCLOSING PARTY a full and fair opportunity, prior to disclosure, to resist, oppose or otherwise limit any disclosure of such CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION. The PARTY will disclose only such information as is legally required and will use commercially reasonable efforts to obtain confidential treatment for any CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION that is s o disclosed.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly stated that a party receiving a court order must provide notice before disclosing confidential information. The changed text removes 'and, in any event, before any CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION is disclosed', weakening this requirement. The contradicted text, which is identical to the original text except for the removed portion, outlines the procedure for handling court orders and the disclosure of confidential information. The omission in the changed text creates a contradiction because it removes the clear directive that notification must occur *before* disclosure, creating ambiguity about the timing of the notification and its relationship to the disclosure. This contradiction makes the agreement less protective of confidential information, which conflicts with the agreement's intent.
